<content>1: package agent
2: 
3: import (

5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"net/url"

12: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
13: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/xdg"
14: )
15: 
16: // ValidationError represents an agent configuration validation failure.
17: type ValidationError struct {
18: 	msg string
19: }
20: 
21: func (e *ValidationError) Error() string {
22: 	return e.msg
23: }
24: 
25: // MemoryConfig holds memory sub-configuration for an agent.
26: type MemoryConfig struct {
27: 	Enabled    bool   `toml:"enabled"`
28: 	Path       string `toml:"path"`
29: 	LastN      int    `toml:"last_n"`
30: 	MaxEntries int    `toml:"max_entries"`
31: }
32: 
33: // ParamsConfig holds model parameter overrides for an agent.
34: type ParamsConfig struct {
35: 	Temperature float64 `toml:"temperature"`
36: 	MaxTokens   int     `toml:"max_tokens"`
37: }
38: 
39: // RetryConfig holds retry sub-configuration for an agent.
40: type RetryConfig struct {
41: 	MaxRetries     int    `toml:"max_retries"`
42: 	Backoff        string `toml:"backoff"`
43: 	InitialDelayMs int    `toml:"initial_delay_ms"`
44: 	MaxDelayMs     int    `toml:"max_delay_ms"`
45: }
46: 
47: // BudgetConfig holds budget sub-configuration for an agent.
48: type BudgetConfig struct {
49: 	MaxTokens int `toml:"max_tokens"`
50: }
51: 
52: // SubAgentsConfig holds sub-agent execution configuration for an agent.
53: type SubAgentsConfig struct {
54: 	MaxDepth int   `toml:"max_depth"`
55: 	Parallel *bool `toml:"parallel"`
56: 	Timeout  int   `toml:"timeout"`
57: }
58: 
59: // MCPServerConfig holds Model Context Protocol server configuration.
60: type MCPServerConfig struct {
61: 	Name      string            `toml:"name"`
62: 	URL       string            `toml:"url"`
63: 	Transport string            `toml:"transport"`
64: 	Headers   map[string]string `toml:"headers"`
65: }
66: 
67: // AgentConfig represents a parsed agent TOML configuration file.
68: type AgentConfig struct {
69: 	Name          string            `toml:"name"`
70: 	Description   string            `toml:"description"`
71: 	Model         string            `toml:"model"`
72: 	SystemPrompt  string            `toml:"system_prompt"`
73: 	Skill         string            `toml:"skill"`
74: 	Files         []string          `toml:"files"`
75: 	Workdir       string            `toml:"workdir"`
76: 	Tools         []string          `toml:"tools"`
77: 	AllowedHosts  []string          `toml:"allowed_hosts"`
78: 	MCPServers    []MCPServerConfig `toml:"mcp_servers"`
79: 	SubAgents     []string          `toml:"sub_agents"`
80: 	SubAgentsConf SubAgentsConfig   `toml:"sub_agents_config"`
81: 	Memory        MemoryConfig      `toml:"memory"`
82: 	Params        ParamsConfig      `toml:"params"`
83: 	Retry         RetryConfig       `toml:"retry"`
84: 	Budget        BudgetConfig      `toml:"budget"`
85: }
86: 
87: // Validate checks that required fields are present in the agent configuration.
88: // It checks name first (fail-fast): if name is missing, it returns that error
89: // without checking model.
90: func Validate(cfg *AgentConfig) error {
91: 	if strings.TrimSpace(cfg.Name) == "" {
92: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "agent config missing required field: name"}
93: 	}
94: 	if strings.TrimSpace(cfg.Model) == "" {
95: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "agent config missing required field: model"}
96: 	}
97: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.MaxDepth < 0 {
98: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "sub_agents_config.max_depth must be non-negative"}
99: 	}
100: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.MaxDepth > 5 {
101: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "sub_agents_config.max_depth cannot exceed 5"}
102: 	}
103: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.Timeout < 0 {
104: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "sub_agents_config.timeout must be non-negative"}
105: 	}
106: 	if cfg.Memory.LastN < 0 {
107: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "memory.last_n must be non-negative"}
108: 	}
109: 	if cfg.Memory.MaxEntries < 0 {
110: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "memory.max_entries must be non-negative"}
111: 	}
112: 	validTools := toolname.ValidNames()
113: 	for _, name := range cfg.Tools {
114: 		if !validTools[name] {
115: 			return &ValidationError{msg: fmt.Sprintf("unknown tool %q in tools config", name)}
116: 		}
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	seenMCPNames := make(map[string]struct{}, len(cfg.MCPServers))
120: 	for i, server := range cfg.MCPServers {
121: 		if strings.TrimSpace(server.Name) == "" {
122: 			return &ValidationError{msg: fmt.Sprintf("mcp_servers[%d].name is required", i)}
123: 		}
124: 		if strings.TrimSpace(server.URL) == "" {
125: 			return &ValidationError{msg: fmt.Sprintf("mcp_servers[%d].url is required", i)}
126: 		}
127: 		parsedURL, err := url.ParseRequestURI(strings.TrimSpace(server.URL))
128: 		if err != nil {
129: 			return &ValidationError{msg: fmt.Sprintf("mcp_servers[%d].url is invalid: %v", i, err)}
130: 		}
131: 		if parsedURL.Scheme != "http" && parsedURL.Scheme != "https" {
132: 			return &ValidationError{msg: fmt.Sprintf("mcp_servers[%d].url must use http or https scheme, got %q", i, parsedURL.Scheme)}
133: 		}
134: 		if parsedURL.Host == "" {
135: 			return &ValidationError{msg: fmt.Sprintf("mcp_servers[%d].url must include a host", i)}
136: 		}
137: 		if server.Transport != "sse" && server.Transport != "streamable-http" {
138: 			return &ValidationError{msg: fmt.Sprintf("mcp_servers[%d].transport must be one of: sse, streamable-http", i)}
139: 		}
140: 		if _, exists := seenMCPNames[server.Name]; exists {
141: 			return &ValidationError{msg: fmt.Sprintf("mcp_servers names must be unique: %q", server.Name)}
142: 		}
143: 		seenMCPNames[server.Name] = struct{}{}
144: 	}
145: 
146: 	if cfg.Retry.MaxRetries < 0 {
147: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "retry.max_retries must be non-negative"}
148: 	}
149: 	if cfg.Retry.Backoff != "" && cfg.Retry.Backoff != "exponential" && cfg.Retry.Backoff != "linear" && cfg.Retry.Backoff != "fixed" {
150: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "retry.backoff must be one of: exponential, linear, fixed"}
151: 	}
152: 	if cfg.Retry.InitialDelayMs < 0 {
153: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "retry.initial_delay_ms must be non-negative"}
154: 	}
155: 	if cfg.Retry.MaxDelayMs < 0 {
156: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "retry.max_delay_ms must be non-negative"}
157: 	}
158: 	if cfg.Retry.InitialDelayMs > 0 && cfg.Retry.MaxDelayMs > 0 && cfg.Retry.MaxDelayMs < cfg.Retry.InitialDelayMs {
159: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "retry.max_delay_ms must be >= retry.initial_delay_ms"}
160: 	}
161: 
162: 	if cfg.Budget.MaxTokens < 0 {
163: 		return &ValidationError{msg: "budget.max_tokens must be non-negative"}
164: 	}
165: 
166: 	return nil
167: }
168: 
169: // loadFromPath reads and parses an agent TOML configuration file from a specific path.
170: // It returns the raw read/parse/validate errors (without any wrapping) so the caller
171: // can handle them appropriately.
172: func loadFromPath(path string) (*AgentConfig, error) {
173: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
174: 	if err != nil {
175: 		return nil, err
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	var cfg AgentConfig
179: 	if _, err := toml.Decode(string(data), &cfg); err != nil {
180: 		return nil, err
181: 	}
182: 
183: 	if err := Validate(&cfg); err != nil {
184: 		return nil, err
185: 	}
186: 
187: 	return &cfg, nil
188: }
189: 
190: // Load reads and parses an agent TOML configuration file by name.
191: // The name parameter is the agent name without the .toml extension.
192: // searchDirs is a list of directories to search first, before falling back to the global XDG config dir.
193: func Load(name string, searchDirs []string) (*AgentConfig, error) {
194: 	// Validate name: must be non-empty and must not contain path separators or traversal sequences
195: 	if strings.TrimSpace(name) == "" {
196: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent name must not be empty")
197: 	}
198: 	if strings.ContainsAny(name, "/\\") || strings.Contains(name, "..") {
199: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent name %q must not contain path separators or traversal sequences", name)
200: 	}
201: 	for _, dir := range searchDirs {
202: 		path := filepath.Join(dir, name+".toml")
203: 		cfg, err := loadFromPath(path)
204: 		if err != nil {
205: 			if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
206: 				continue // file doesn't exist in this dir, try next
207: 			}
208: 			// File exists but failed to load (parse error, validation error, etc.)
209: 			// Check if it's a validation error - pass through unchanged
210: 			var valErr *ValidationError
211: 			if errors.As(err, &valErr) {
212: 				return nil, err
213: 			}
214: 			// Wrap other errors with context
215: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse agent config %q: %w", name, err)
216: 		}
217: 		return cfg, nil
218: 	}
219: 
220: 	// Fall back to global XDG config dir
221: 	configDir, err := xdg.GetConfigDir()
222: 	if err != nil {
223: 		return nil, err
224: 	}
225: 	path := filepath.Join(configDir, "agents", name+".toml")
226: 	cfg, err := loadFromPath(path)
227: 	if err != nil {
228: 		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
229: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent config not found: %s", name)
230: 		}
231: 		var valErr *ValidationError
232: 		if errors.As(err, &valErr) {
233: 			return nil, err
234: 		}
235: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse agent config %q: %w", name, err)
236: 	}
237: 	return cfg, nil
238: }
239: 
240: // List returns all valid agent configurations from the agents directory.
241: // Invalid files are silently skipped. If the agents directory does not exist,
242: // an empty slice is returned.
243: // searchDirs is a list of directories to search first, before falling back to the global XDG config dir.
244: func List(searchDirs []string) ([]AgentConfig, error) {
245: 	seen := make(map[string]struct{})
246: 	var agents []AgentConfig
247: 
248: 	// Collect all directories to search: searchDirs first, then global XDG dir
249: 	var allDirs []string
250: 	allDirs = append(allDirs, searchDirs...)
251: 
252: 	configDir, err := xdg.GetConfigDir()
253: 	if err != nil {
254: 		return nil, err
255: 	}
256: 	allDirs = append(allDirs, filepath.Join(configDir, "agents"))
257: 
258: 	// Process directories in order (earlier = higher precedence)
259: 	for _, agentsDir := range allDirs {
260: 		entries, err := os.ReadDir(agentsDir)
261: 		if err != nil {
262: 			// Silently skip non-existent directories
263: 			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
264: 				continue
265: 			}
266: 			return nil, err
267: 		}
268: 
269: 		for _, entry := range entries {
270: 			if entry.IsDir() {
271: 				continue
272: 			}
273: 			if !strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".toml") {
274: 				continue
275: 			}
276: 			name := strings.TrimSuffix(entry.Name(), ".toml")
277: 
278: 			// Skip if we've already seen this agent name (first occurrence wins)
279: 			if _, exists := seen[name]; exists {
280: 				continue
281: 			}
282: 
283: 			// Try to load from this specific path
284: 			path := filepath.Join(agentsDir, entry.Name())
285: 			cfg, err := loadFromPath(path)
286: 			if err != nil {
287: 				continue // skip invalid files
288: 			}
289: 
290: 			seen[name] = struct{}{}
291: 			agents = append(agents, *cfg)
292: 		}
293: 	}
294: 
295: 	return agents, nil
296: }
297: 
298: // Scaffold returns a TOML template string for a new agent configuration.
299: // The name argument is interpolated into the template.
300: func Scaffold(name string) (string, error) {
301: 	tmpl := `name = "` + name + `"
302: description = ""
303: 
304: # Full provider/model per models.dev
305: model = "provider/model-name"
306: 
307: # Agent persona (optional)
308: # system_prompt = ""
309: 
310: # Default skill (optional, can be overridden with --skill flag)
311: # skill = ""
312: 
313: # Context files - glob patterns resolved from workdir or cwd (optional)
314: # files = []
315: 
316: # Working directory (optional)
317: # workdir = ""
318: 
319: # Tools this agent can use (optional)
320: # Valid: list_directory, read_file, write_file, edit_file, run_command, url_fetch, web_search
321: # tools = []
322: 
323: # Restrict url_fetch to these hostnames (optional, empty = allow all)
324: # allowed_hosts = []
325: 
326: # Sub-agents this agent can invoke (optional)
327: # sub_agents = []
328: 
329: # [sub_agents_config]
330: # max_depth = 3
331: # parallel = true
332: # timeout = 120
333: 
334: # MCP server connections (optional)
335: # [[mcp_servers]]
336: # name = "my-tools"
337: # url = "https://my-mcp-server.example.com/sse"
338: # transport = "sse"
339: # headers = { Authorization = "Bearer ${MY_TOKEN}" }
340: 
341: # [memory]
342: # enabled = false
343: # path = ""
344: # last_n = 10
345: # max_entries = 100
346: 
347: # [params]
348: # temperature = 0.3
349: # max_tokens = 4096
350: 
351: # [retry]
352: # max_retries = 0
353: # backoff = "exponential"
354: # initial_delay_ms = 500
355: # max_delay_ms = 30000
356: 
357: # [budget]
358: # max_tokens = 0
359: `
360: 	return tmpl, nil
361: }
362: 
363: // BuildSearchDirs builds the search directories slice from a flag value and base directory.
364: // If flagDir is non-empty, it is appended as the first element.
365: // The auto-discovery path (baseDir/axe/agents) is always appended.
366: func BuildSearchDirs(flagDir string, baseDir string) []string {
367: 	var dirs []string
368: 	if flagDir != "" {
369: 		dirs = append(dirs, flagDir)
370: 	}
371: 	dirs = append(dirs, filepath.Join(baseDir, "axe", "agents"))
372: 	return dirs
373: }
374: 
375: // tomlDecode is a package-level wrapper for toml.Decode, used by tests.
376: var tomlDecode = toml.Decode

(End of file - total 376 lines)
